pattern: Writing the health after entering room i starting from room j as hp plus a prefix-sum difference turns "does this room award a point" into a comparison between a prefix sum and a threshold that depends only on the room, and prefix sums of positive damages are already sorted, so counting qualifying starts becomes a binary search.
hints: Precompute prefix sums of damage; the health remaining after entering room i, having started at room j, can be written purely in terms of those two prefix sums and hp.

For a fixed room i, rearrange the "you get a point" condition so that everything depending on the starting room j is isolated on one side and everything depending only on i is on the other -- this turns each room into a threshold on the starting prefix sum.

Because every damage value is positive, the prefix sums are strictly increasing, so counting how many valid starting rooms clear a room's threshold is a single binary search rather than a scan; sum those counts over every room.

edge_inputs.description: statement example two, health goes deeply negative but the journey still completes
single room that is always cleared
single room that is never cleared
remaining health lands exactly on the requirement, which must still count as a pass
an unreachable requirement means every starting room scores zero
single room at the maximum allowed values
minimum hp with minimum damage and requirement

stress.description: random moderate-length runs with varied hp
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(size, 2000))
    hp = rng.randint(1, 1000000000)
    damage = [rng.randint(1, 10000) for _ in range(n)]
    requirement = [rng.randint(1, 10000) for _ in range(n)]
    return [hp, damage, requirement]

stress.description: small hp against large damage values, so most rooms fail
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(size, 1500))
    hp = rng.randint(1, 1000)
    damage = [rng.randint(5000, 10000) for _ in range(n)]
    requirement = [rng.randint(1, 10000) for _ in range(n)]
    return [hp, damage, requirement]
